meta.methodology: HTTP API measurements via urllib.request. 10-iteration warmup discarded. Nearest-rank percentiles. No outlier trimming.
meta.caveat: Measurements on self-hosted Hostinger VPS. Network conditions differ from SaaS providers. Direct comparison with Zep Cloud / Mem0 Cloud numbers not apples-to-apples (their latency = SaaS infra, ours = VPS).

standard_hybrid_n100.description: Mixed short+medium queries, FTS5 + Gemini embed + RRF, production corpus 69k chunks

long_queries_n80.description: 10+ word NL queries (full BM25+semantic pipeline), warm corpus

kg_path_only_n120.description: KG graph walk only (no embed, pure SQLite BFS) — $0/query
kg_path_only_n120.note: KG path is SQLite-only. Zero API calls. Dominates when entities are pre-indexed.

kg_plus_hybrid_n15.description: KG walk + hybrid search sequential (worst-case combined)
kg_plus_hybrid_n15.note: KG walk adds ~3ms on top of hybrid (negligible overhead). Dominant cost = Gemini embed call.

latency_breakdown.note: Gemini embed round-trip from Hostinger VPS (BR) to Google US-East is ~400-600ms. Self-hosting a local embed model (e.g. nomic-embed-text via Ollama) would reduce p50 to ~15-50ms at cost of quality degradation.

footprint.note_341mb_paper: PR #283 paper reported 341MB RSS. Current measurement 399MB reflects corpus growth (62k → 69k chunks) + 7 days additional KG growth since paper measurement.

throughput.bottleneck: Gemini embed API rate limiting + single Node.js event loop. Parallelism bounded by Gemini quota (default: 1500 RPM = 25 RPS max embed).
throughput.note: Throughput scales with Gemini API quota. At 25 RPS embed quota, theoretical max = ~25 concurrent queries/sec. Current VPS is I/O-bound on embed, not CPU.

zep_cloud.caveat: SaaS latency; nox-mem is self-hosted VPS — comparison not apples-to-apples

mem0_cloud.source: mem0.ai docs ('optimized for <200ms'), no percentile breakdown

memos.source: not published — no latency benchmark in github.com/MemTensor/MemOS

zep_oss.source: not published (self-hosted requires Postgres + vector extension)

mem0_oss.source: not published (Python, depends on embed provider)

memos.source: not published — self-hosted, embed cost user-provided

langmem.source: not published — library, no separate pricing

zep_oss.note: Requires Postgres (separate process); combined footprint not published

mem0_oss.note: Python process; not published. Estimated 50-200MB for lightweight workloads.

letta.note: Requires Postgres + Qdrant; combined stack significantly heavier than nox-mem

self_hosted.nox_mem: yes — Node.js + SQLite, single process, no external services required

backbone_portable.nox_mem: yes — Gemini / OpenAI / Anthropic (switchable via env)

honest_gaps.latency: nox-mem p50=529ms is dominated by Gemini embed API round-trip (~400-600ms Hostinger BR → Google US). Zep Cloud '<100ms' claim (if accurate) benefits from co-located SaaS infra. With local embed model, nox-mem would reach ~15-50ms p50. Direct latency comparison requires same embed setup.
honest_gaps.throughput: At 1.67 qps serial (embed-bottlenecked), nox-mem is not suitable for high-traffic SaaS workloads without either: (a) Gemini quota increase, (b) local embed model, or (c) embed caching layer.
honest_gaps.memory: 399MB RSS is lightweight for a 69k-chunk corpus. However Letta/Zep OSS also require Postgres which adds 200-500MB separately — nox-mem's single-process model is a genuine advantage.
honest_gaps.kg_path: 2.53ms p50 KG path is a genuine competitive advantage — zero API cost, zero latency overhead vs Gemini. But KG coverage (402 entities) is sparse vs dense vector search. PR #384 confirmed dense KG hurts quality.
honest_gaps.cost: Mem0 Cloud $0.001/call vs nox-mem $0.0000013/query embed = 769× cheaper on retrieval cost. But Mem0 Cloud includes managed infra; nox-mem requires VPS (~$12/mo). Break-even at >14k queries/mo.
honest_gaps.benchmark_gap: No published latency benchmarks from MemOS, LangMem, or Letta for retrieval-only workloads. Comparison is nox-mem measured vs competitor unverified claims.
